instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-string` that evaluates a value once and signals an error if it is not a string. Returns the value when valid.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 NAME))
            (UNLESS (STRINGP #:G1) (ERROR "Expected a string, got ~S" #:G1))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 NAME))
            (UNLESS (STRINGP #:G112)
              (ERROR "Expected a string, got ~S" #:G112))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
